headline: Exxon, Chevron, big oil send signals ahead of earnings
summary: For the most part, 2026 has been a very good year for oil giants and the not-so-giant oil companies. Oil prices are up substantially since the start of the year: 67% for light sweet crude, the benchmark U.S. oil, and 78% for Brent, the global benchmark. Which means, of course, that more of the ...

headline: Is It Too Late To Consider Exxon Mobil (XOM) After A 41% One-Year Rally?
summary: For investors considering whether Exxon Mobil at around US$148 per share still offers value, or if most of the opportunity is already reflected in the price, this article examines what the current market price might be implying. The stock has returned 0.3% over the last 7 days, 20.8% year to date and 41.1% over the past year. It has also seen a 13.3% decline over the last 30 days, which may have influenced how the market views its risk and reward profile. Recent coverage has focused on Exxon...

headline: Exxon and Chevron Stock Jump. BP Doubles Profit on ‘Exceptional’ Oil Trading.
summary: BP  stock rose Tuesday after the rally in crude prices helped the British oil major to double its first-quarter profit.  BP’s American depositary receipts climbed 1% to $46.40.  The results cover a period where the Iran war disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, sparking a surge in oil prices.

headline: COP Stock Looks Undervalued Ahead of Q1 Results: Time to Buy?
summary: COP to report Q1 on April 30. Estimates imply lower EPS and revenue, but shares are up 32% and trade at an EV/EBITDA discount.

headline: Is Wall Street Bullish or Bearish on Exxon Mobil Stock?
summary: Exxon Mobil has outperformed the S&P 500 Index over the past year, and Wall Street analysts are still cautiously upbeat about its outlook going forward.

headline: ValuEngine Weekly Market Summary And Commentary
summary: Despite geopolitical friction, the stock market enters the final week of April at record highs fueled by Mag7 earnings optimism and hopes for a diplomatic resolution to the US-Iran stalemate

headline: BP Stock Rises After Huge Jump in Profit. What It Means for Exxon and Chevron.
summary: BP  stock was rising on Tuesday after the rally in crude prices helped the British oil major to double its first-quarter profit.  BP’s American depositary receipts climbed 3.7% to $47.65 ahead of the opening bell.  The results cover a period where the war in the Middle East disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, sparking a surge in oil prices.

headline: BP Profit Jumps as Oil Trading Boomed During Iran War
summary: (Bloomberg) -- BP Plc’s earnings far exceeded analyst expectations in the first quarter as spiraling energy prices and market turmoil caused by the Iran war led to a surge in profits from its oil trading operation.
